name: Duck with Black Bean Sauce and Tamarind Jus
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the stuffing ingredients in the cavity of each duck.
2. Trim excess fat and bind the legs at the tail end.
3. Season with salt and pepper; roast in a 325°F oven for 2 hours.
4. Pour off any grease when ducks are golden brown.
5. Remove from oven and cool to room temperature.
6. Halve the ducks along the backbone and debone; discard stuffing.
7. Add bones to 4 cups of water with an onion, carrot, salt, and pepper; simmer for about 2 hours. Reserve duck stock.
8. Black bean sauce: Blanch beans until soft, chop coarsely; puree skin apple. Sweat shallots in port until translucent. Combine beans, pureed apple, and remaining ingredients (except butter) with port; simmer 15 minutes. Stir in butter; keep warm.
9. Tamarind jus: Soak tamarind until soft, squeeze seed out, strain pulp. Puree pulp and duck stock; heat through. Stir in butter.
10. Place skin side down in a roasting pan; roast at 500°F for about 10 minutes (until crispy). Pour black bean sauce over plates; top ducks skin side up. Drizzle tamarind jus over ducks; serve with oriental vegetables.
